Petition For Determination Of Persons Entitled To Distributions-"Antiquities" And Order
Off Calendar Aug-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Wayne McIntosh for and with Rebecca McIntosh.

The Court asked if there was any legal authority for the hypothetical trust. Mr. McIntosh stated that he is in agreement with the examiner, that all of the information is before the court, that there is a difficult and long history regarding the items, that they spent $5,000.00 for an appraisal to find that they are clever fakes and when the estate was being drafted he was told that they were valuable items. Mr. McIntosh further stated that the proposed order is 100% in accord with the probate examiner and that he believes the items should be sold to defray the cost of the appraisal. The Court asked if the University would want to purchase the items and if a notice of proposed action was necessary. Mr. McIntosh stated that they would not want to pay for them, that a copy of the petition was served on the University, that he received a letter from the University stating that they could use the items for teaching purposes, that he wrote back stating that the estate would like to sell the items to recoup expenses and that the University acknowledged this, that they have a proof of service that the petition was served on the University and that no one is objecting. The Court took the petition for determination of persons entitled to distribution under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For Determination Of Persons Entitled To Distributions-"Antiquities" And Order
Off Calendar May-11-2015 Continued To Jun-08-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Wayne McIntosh for and with (administrator) Rebecca McIntosh.

The Court stated that there are issues with what has been filed and that a 4th amended petition addressing issues in the examiners notes will be necessary. Mr. McIntosh stated that his declaration addressed the issues, that there was nothing substantial in the examiners notes, that he filed the 3rd amended accounting to address the Australian complications, that he thought all issues had been addressed and has received no further notes from the examiner. The Court stated that further notes were generated after the declaration was filed and that the 4th amended should restate and supersede the other accountings filed and address receipts, disbursements, distributions, ending assets on hand and bond. Mr. McIntosh stated that he was under the impression that the examiner was satisfied, that the only outstanding issue is the antiquities and that $2,000.00 worth of antiquities is holding up a $1,000,000.00 estate. The Petition for Determination of Persons Entitled to Distributions, etc., the Petition to Modify (Reduce) Bond and the Accounting petition were continued to June 8, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court is to clarify the issues with the Probate Examiner and Mr. McIntosh will be notified if this date goes off calendar.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-19-2014 Continued To Dec-22-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Wayne McIntosh for and with Rebecca McIntosh (Administrator).

Mr. McIntosh stated that he agrees with the Probate Examiner, that it would be easier to separate/split administration of this Estate and the Australia Estate and asked for separate orders carving out some of the issues. The Court informed Counsel that he needs to file an amended inventory and appraisal, amend the account and amend the request for fees. The Court also advised Counsel that it is not sure if it is appropriate to issue separate orders. Mr. McIntosh will submit proposed orders for the Court's review/consideration. The accounting petition was continued to December 22, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
